books.title: Dune
books.author: Frank Herbert
books.description: A stunning blend of adventure and mysticism, environmentalism and politics. Dune won the first Nebula Award and shared the Hugo Award.

books.author: George Orwell
books.description: Among the seminal texts of the 20th century, 1984 is a rare work that grows more haunting as its futuristic vision becomes mirrored in modern reality.

books.title: Atomic Habits
books.author: James Clear
books.description: No matter your goals, Atomic Habits offers a proven framework for improving every day. James Clear reveals practical strategies to form good habits, break bad ones.

books.title: The Alchemist
books.author: Paulo Coelho
books.description: The magical story of Santiago, an Andalusian shepherd boy who yearns to travel in search of a worldly treasure as extravagant as any ever found.

books.title: Rich Dad Poor Dad
books.author: Robert Kiyosaki
books.description: Rich Dad Poor Dad is Robert's story of growing up with two dads and the ways in which both men shaped his thoughts about money and investing.

books.title: The Silent Patient
books.author: Alex Michaelides
books.description: Alicia Berenson's life is seemingly perfect. A famous painter married to an in-demand fashion photographer, she lives in a grand house. Then one day she shoots her husband.

books.title: Pride and Prejudice
books.author: Jane Austen
books.description: Since its immediate success in 1813, Pride and Prejudice has remained one of the most popular novels in the English language. Jane Austen called this her 'own darling child'.

books.title: Sapiens
books.author: Yuval Noah Harari
books.description: A groundbreaking narrative of humanity's creation and evolution—a #1 international bestseller—that explores the ways in which biology and history have defined us.

books.title: The Midnight Library
books.author: Matt Haig
books.description: Between life and death there is a library, and within that library, the shelves go on forever. Every book provides a chance to try another life you could have lived.

books.title: Steve Jobs
books.author: Walter Isaacson
books.description: Based on more than forty interviews with Jobs conducted over two years—as well as interviews with more than 100 family members, friends, adversaries, and colleagues.

books.title: The Hunger Games
books.author: Suzanne Collins
books.description: In the ruins of a place once known as North America lies the nation of Panem. In punishment for a past rebellion, each district must provide one boy and one girl to compete.

books.title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
books.author: Daniel Kahneman
books.description: Kahneman takes us on a groundbreaking tour of the mind and explains the two systems that drive the way we think. System 1 is fast, intuitive, and emotional; System 2 is slower.

books.title: The Great Gatsby
books.author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
books.description: The Great Gatsby stands as the supreme achievement of Fitzgerald's career. This exemplary novel of the Jazz Age has been acclaimed by generations of readers.

books.title: Gone Girl
books.author: Gillian Flynn
books.description: On a warm summer morning in North Carthage, Missouri, it is Nick and Amy Dunne's fifth wedding anniversary when Nick's clever and beautiful wife disappears.

books.title: Educated
books.author: Tara Westover
books.description: Tara Westover was 17 the first time she set foot in a classroom. Born to survivalists in the mountains of Idaho, she prepared for the end of the world by stockpiling home-canned peaches.

books.title: The Body
books.author: Bill Bryson
books.description: Bill Bryson takes us on a grand tour of the most complex and miraculous thing in creation — the human body. Bryson shows us how it works and its remarkable ability to heal itself.
